g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about Q1: What elements should AkChem consider when moving to sustainable chemicals?')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about Q2: Which sustainable pathway should AkChem prioritize?')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about Q3: Site closure and financial viability analysis')
sendPrompt('What external drivers are pushing AkChem toward sustainable chemicals?')
sendPrompt('What internal readiness factors must AkChem assess before transitioning?')
sendPrompt('What regulatory and market drivers (carbon pricing, EU packaging rules, customer demand) push AkChem toward sustainable chemicals?')
sendPrompt('What competitive cost pressures (energy, CO2, overcapacity) are squeezing AkChem margins?')
sendPrompt('How ready are AkChems existing assets and production capabilities for a sustainable transition?')
sendPrompt('What strategic risks (execution, technology, financing) must AkChem manage in the transition?')
sendPrompt('How do we assess market attractiveness for each sustainable chemical pathway?')
sendPrompt('How do we assess technology readiness and feedstock feasibility for each pathway?')
sendPrompt('How do we evaluate strategic fit with AkChems existing assets for each pathway?')
sendPrompt('What is the market size, growth, and demand trajectory for recycling-based, bio-based, and CO2-based chemicals?')
sendPrompt('What premium pricing and margin uplift can sustainable chemicals command vs fossil-based?')
sendPrompt('How mature is the technology for mechanical recycling, chemical recycling, bio-based, and CO2-based pathways?')
sendPrompt('How available and cost-competitive are feedstocks for each sustainable pathway in Europe?')
sendPrompt('How much CapEx and lead time does each pathway require, and how does that fit AkChems balance sheet?')
sendPrompt('What is the GHG and circularity value proposition of each pathway for AkChem customers?')
sendPrompt('How do we value the NPV of keeping each AkChem site in fossil-based production?')
sendPrompt('How do we build the DCF model for switching a site to sustainable chemicals?')
sendPrompt('What are the DCF cash flows and NPV of continuing fossil-based production at Antwerp, Hamburg, and Barcelona?')
sendPrompt('What one-off closure costs (severance, decommissioning, remediation) apply if a site is shut?')
sendPrompt('How do we model incremental revenue and NOPAT uplift from switching to sustainable chemicals per site?')
sendPrompt('How do we model CapEx (EUR 250M + 4.5% annual) and subsidies (grants, CCfD, green loans) in the switch DCF?')

svg.text: → urgency is high; risks are real
and close if both are negative
DCF model logic (Q3 — the financial crux)
2026 revenue = avg(2023–25). Fossil: grow 1%/yr, EBIT margin per site, CapEx 4.5%/rev from 2026.
Switch: EUR 250M outflow 2026, grow 4%/yr, sustainable EBIT margin, CapEx 4.5%/rev from 2027.
FCF = NOPAT − CapEx (D&A = ΔNWC so net zero). Discount to 2026 at site WACC. Horizon: 2050.
ΔNPV = NPV(switch) − NPV(fossil). If ΔNPV > 0 → switch viable. Also compare vs closure costs.
Prioritize chemical recycling. Switch sites where ΔNPV(switch − fossil) > 0 (Hamburg likely most attractive
given largest +5pp margin uplift). Close only if both fossil NPV and switch NPV are negative vs. closure costs.
Click any box to explore further ↗
MECE: Q1 = external/internal context · Q2 = pathway choice · Q3 = financial decision
